rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLEN J. CHUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Accountings On Minors' Trusts
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel to report (1) the reason for this delay closing the estate; (2) why a 2-year accounting has not been filed despite the court's previous order; (3) status of the accounting for the minors' trust; and (4) regarding the failure to appear at the last hearing on 2/15/2023 . PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAURICE BASSAN 2004 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Order Denying Motion For Judgment Against David Bassan
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Petitioner Lou Ann Bassan's Motion for Reconsideration (Order Denying Motion for Judgment Against David Bassan) is DENIED. Opposing party David Bassan is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIRLEY S.M. CHONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/25/2023 to be heard with the related trust case (PTR-22-305356). 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. 2) No proof of service of summons with a copy of the objection and contest of the 2021 will is on file as to Cindy Chong, Sophia Chong (if she is at least 12 years of age), and Peter Chong (if he is at least 12 years of age). See Probate Code sections 8250(a), 8110(b), and 1208(b). See also CCP section 416.60. 3) No answer or other responsive pleading has been filed. See section 8251. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANG WU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/12/2023. (1) Petition needs to clarify the interests of the post-deceased spouseâs estate and to propose distribution accordingly. Declaration filed 2/9/2023 seeks the distribution to bypass the estate of Koon Hai Wu for judicial economy; however, statutory instruction under PC sec. 11802(b) and (c) should be considered first. If PC sec. 11802(c) is applicable here, please file the affidavits conventionally. LRSF 14.59.C.(2). ( 2) Proposed Order must be submitted mirroring the above information. (3) Waiver of account needs to be filed by Wieland Wu. Please file all required documents two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT A. PATERSON, JR. AND CHRISTINE E. PATERSON TRUST DATED AUGUST 24, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Determining Validity Of Trust Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsels to update the court with the status of the case. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY CHONG 2016 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition 1. To Determine Validity Of Purported 2021 Trust 2. For Financial Elder Abuse For Payment Of Attorneys Fees For Compensatory And Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/25/2023. 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 2) Re each minor beneficiary entitled to notice: i) The date of birth must be provided. ii) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor entitled to notice under CRC 7.51(d) must be identified. iii) No guardian ad litem has been appointed for any minor beneficiary. 3) Is the financial elder abuse claim a related cause of action that must be pleaded in a compulsory cross-complaint to be filed in the related civil action? See CCP sections 426.10(c) & 426.30(a). 4) If the financial elder abuse claim is asserted against Cindy Chong (who is neither a beneficiary nor the first-named trustee under the 2021 restatement), she must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 17203(b). Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY CHONG 2016 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To (1) Remove Albert Chong As Successor Trustee Of The Shirley Chong 2016 Trust, (2) Order Any Successor Trustee To Comply With The Terms
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/25/2023. 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-120 form with a proof of service is on file. A copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Re each minor beneficiary identified in paragraph VII of the petition: i) The date of birth must be provided. ii) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor entitled to notice under CRC 7.51(d) must be identified. iii) No guardian guardian ad litem has been appointed for any minor beneficiary. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANGELO MARTINEZ SENDEJAS LVING TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 29, 2004
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/26/2023. (1) Heirs at law who are not the trust beneficiaries must have been served in the matter PC 17203(b), personal service. Notice filed 2/17/2023 shows that additional persons from #6, Johnny to #15, Annette, who may be the heirs whose interests may be affected by this petition, were served by mail. Also, mailing service did not meet the 30-day requirement. (2) Petition is silent when the property (Clearlake property) was purchased/acquired by the decedent. If the property was acquired after the trust/general assignment was executed, the decedent did not have the interest to assign at the time of execution. A verified supplement making further allegation with a copy of the current vesting to be filed. If the property was purchased after the decedent's testamentary documents were executed, petitioner may need to present an authority under which the court can grant the request. (3) A verified supplement stating the heirs at law for the court to review if all interested parties are properly noticed. Examiner's continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
